   310	def build_daily_etf_execution_frame(
   311	    etf_daily: pl.LazyFrame | pl.DataFrame,
   312	    adj_factors: pl.LazyFrame | pl.DataFrame | None = None,
   313	    premium_discounts: pl.LazyFrame | pl.DataFrame | None = None,
   314	    *,
   315	    horizons: tuple[int, ...] = EXECUTION_HORIZONS,
   316	    max_abs_premium_discount_bps: float = 100.0,
   317	) -> pl.LazyFrame:
   318	    names = _schema_names(etf_daily)
   319	    missing = sorted(set(ETF_REQUIRED_COLUMNS) - names)
   320	    if missing:
   321	        raise ValueError(f"ETF daily panel missing columns: {missing}")
   322	    lf = etf_daily.lazy() if isinstance(etf_daily, pl.DataFrame) else etf_daily
   323	    out = (
   324	        lf.select(_existing(names, ("ts_code", "trade_date", "pre_close", "open", "high", "low", "close", "vol", "amount")))
   325	        .with_columns(pl.col("ts_code").cast(pl.Utf8), _normalize_date_expr("trade_date").alias("trade_date"))
   326	        .sort(["ts_code", "trade_date"])
   327	    )
   328	    if adj_factors is not None:
   329	        adj_lf = adj_factors.lazy() if isinstance(adj_factors, pl.DataFrame) else adj_factors
   330	        adj_names = _schema_names(adj_factors)
   331	        if {"ts_code", "trade_date", "adj_factor"}.issubset(adj_names):
   332	            adj = adj_lf.select(["ts_code", "trade_date", "adj_factor"]).with_columns(
   333	                pl.col("ts_code").cast(pl.Utf8),
   334	                _normalize_date_expr("trade_date").alias("trade_date"),
   335	                pl.col("adj_factor").cast(pl.Float64),
   336	            )
   337	            out = out.join(adj, on=["ts_code", "trade_date"], how="left")
   338	    if "adj_factor" not in _schema_names(out):
   339	        out = out.with_columns(pl.lit(None, dtype=pl.Float64).alias("adj_factor"))
   340	    out = out.with_columns(
   341	        (pl.col("adj_factor").is_not_null() & (pl.col("adj_factor") > 0)).alias("adj_factor_available"),
   342	        pl.when(pl.col("adj_factor").is_not_null() & (pl.col("adj_factor") > 0))
   343	        .then(pl.col("open") * pl.col("adj_factor"))
   344	        .otherwise(pl.col("open"))
   345	        .alias("adj_open"),
   346	        pl.when(pl.col("adj_factor").is_not_null() & (pl.col("adj_factor") > 0))
   347	        .then(pl.col("high") * pl.col("adj_factor"))
   348	        .otherwise(pl.col("high"))
   349	        .alias("adj_high"),
   350	        pl.when(pl.col("adj_factor").is_not_null() & (pl.col("adj_factor") > 0))
   351	        .then(pl.col("low") * pl.col("adj_factor"))
   352	        .otherwise(pl.col("low"))
   353	        .alias("adj_low"),
   354	        pl.when(pl.col("adj_factor").is_not_null() & (pl.col("adj_factor") > 0))
   355	        .then(pl.col("close") * pl.col("adj_factor"))
   356	        .otherwise(pl.col("close"))
   357	        .alias("adj_close"),
   358	        (pl.col("amount") * 1000.0).alias("amount_cny"),
   359	    )
   360	    if premium_discounts is not None:
   361	        premium_lf = premium_discounts.lazy() if isinstance(premium_discounts, pl.DataFrame) else premium_discounts
   362	        premium_names = _schema_names(premium_discounts)
   363	        if {"ts_code", "trade_date", "premium_discount_bps"}.issubset(premium_names):
   364	            premium = premium_lf.select(["ts_code", "trade_date", "premium_discount_bps"]).with_columns(
   365	                pl.col("ts_code").cast(pl.Utf8),
   366	                _normalize_date_expr("trade_date").alias("trade_date"),
   367	                pl.col("premium_discount_bps").cast(pl.Float64),
   368	            )
   369	            out = out.join(premium, on=["ts_code", "trade_date"], how="left")
   370	    if "premium_discount_bps" not in _schema_names(out):
   371	        out = out.with_columns(pl.lit(None, dtype=pl.Float64).alias("premium_discount_bps"))
   372	    out = out.with_columns(
   373	        pl.when(pl.col("adj_factor_available") & pl.col("premium_discount_bps").is_not_null())
   374	        .then(pl.lit("full_eod_reference"))
   375	        .when(pl.col("adj_factor_available"))
   376	        .then(pl.lit("adjusted_price_only"))
   377	        .otherwise(pl.lit("raw_price_only_unadjusted"))
   378	        .alias("etf_reference_status"),
   379	        (
   380	            pl.col("open").is_not_null()
   381	            & pl.col("close").is_not_null()
   382	            & (pl.col("amount").fill_null(0.0) > 0)
   383	            & pl.col("adj_factor_available")
   384	            & (pl.col("premium_discount_bps").is_null() | (pl.col("premium_discount_bps").abs() <= max_abs_premium_discount_bps))
   385	        ).alias("etf_whitelist_eligible"),
   386	    )
   387	    out = out.sort(["ts_code", "trade_date"]).with_columns(
   388	        _forward_columns(horizons, ("trade_date", "open", "close", "adj_open", "adj_close"))
   389	    )
   390	    return out.with_columns(
   391	        _concat_reasons(
   392	            [
   393	                (pl.col("open").is_null() | pl.col("close").is_null(), "missing_price"),
   394	                (pl.col("amount").fill_null(0.0) <= 0, "missing_or_zero_amount"),
   395	                (pl.col("adj_factor_available").not_(), "adj_factor_unavailable_raw_price_only"),
   396	                (pl.col("premium_discount_bps").abs() > max_abs_premium_discount_bps, "abnormal_eod_premium_discount"),
   397	            ],
   398	            "etf_execution_reason_codes",
   399	        )
   400	    )

   403	def build_rebalance_calendar(trade_dates: pd.Series | list[str]) -> pd.DataFrame:
   404	    dates = pd.Series(trade_dates, dtype="string").dropna().astype(str).str.replace(r"\.0$", "", regex=True).drop_duplicates().sort_values()
   405	    frame = pd.DataFrame({"trade_date": dates.to_list()})
   406	    frame["trade_date_dt"] = pd.to_datetime(frame["trade_date"], format="%Y%m%d", errors="coerce")
   407	    frame = frame.dropna(subset=["trade_date_dt"]).sort_values("trade_date_dt").reset_index(drop=True)
   408	    frame["next_trade_date"] = frame["trade_date"].shift(-1).fillna("")
   409	    frame["calendar_year_week"] = frame["trade_date_dt"].dt.strftime("%G%V")
   410	    frame["calendar_month"] = frame["trade_date_dt"].dt.strftime("%Y%m")
   411	    frame["is_daily_rebalance"] = True
   412	    frame["is_weekly_rebalance"] = ~frame["calendar_year_week"].duplicated()
   413	    frame["is_monthly_rebalance"] = ~frame["calendar_month"].duplicated()
   414	    long_frames = []
   415	    for cadence, mask in (
   416	        ("daily", frame["is_daily_rebalance"]),
   417	        ("weekly", frame["is_weekly_rebalance"]),
   418	        ("monthly", frame["is_monthly_rebalance"]),
   419	    ):
   420	        part = frame.loc[mask, ["trade_date", "next_trade_date"]].copy()
   421	        part["cadence"] = cadence
   422	        long_frames.append(part)
   423	    out = pd.concat(long_frames, ignore_index=True)
   424	    out = out.rename(columns={"trade_date": "signal_date", "next_trade_date": "rebalance_date"})
   425	    ordered = out[["cadence", "signal_date", "rebalance_date"]].reset_index(drop=True)
   426	    return cast(pd.DataFrame, ordered)

   429	def build_execution_manifest(
   430	    input_paths: dict[str, Path],
   431	    output_paths: dict[str, Path],
   432	    *,
   433	    output_label: str,
   434	    stock_rows: int,
   435	    etf_rows: int,
   436	    calendar_rows: int,
   437	    stock_date_range: dict[str, str],
   438	    etf_date_range: dict[str, str],
   439	    etf_reference_coverage: dict[str, float],
   440	) -> dict[str, Any]:
   441	    def file_info(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
   442	        return {
   443	            "path": str(path),
   444	            "exists": path.exists(),
   445	            "size_bytes": path.stat().st_size if path.exists() else 0,
   446	            "sha256": file_sha256(path) if path.exists() and path.is_file() else "",
   447	        }
   448	
   449	    return {
   450	        "output_label": output_label,
   451	        "contract_versions": {"execution_ready": "v1"},
   452	        "files": {name: file_info(path) for name, path in input_paths.items()},
   453	        "outputs": {name: file_info(path) for name, path in output_paths.items()},
   454	        "row_counts": {
   455	            "daily_stock_execution": stock_rows,
   456	            "daily_etf_execution": etf_rows,
   457	            "rebalance_calendar": calendar_rows,
   458	        },
   459	        "date_ranges": {"stock_daily": stock_date_range, "etf_daily": etf_date_range},
   460	        "policy_versions": {
   461	            "source_boundary": "curated_daily_panels_plus_gated_monthly_ml_ready",
   462	            "daily_weekly_research_gate": "requires_quant_gate_execution_ready_dataset",
   463	            "stock_price_basis": "raw_ohlc_plus_same_day_adjusted_ohlc",
   464	            "stock_moneyflow_policy": "propagate_curated_daily_order_size_moneyflow_when_available",
   465	            "stock_pit_industry_policy": "latest_known_capco_csrc_l1_by_trade_date_when_available",
   466	            "etf_price_basis": "adjusted_when_tushare_fund_adj_available_else_raw_diagnostic_only",
   467	            "etf_raw_price_only_policy": "diagnostic_only_not_promotable",
   468	            "premium_discount_policy": "eod_nav_diagnostic_when_available",
   469	        },
   470	        "coverage": {"etf_reference": etf_reference_coverage},
   471	    }

   474	def _read_manifest(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
   475	    if not path.exists():
   476	        return {}
   477	    return json.loads(path.read_text())

   480	def _issue(issues: list[GateIssue], severity: str, code: str, message: str) -> None:
   481	    issues.append(GateIssue(severity=severity, code=code, message=message))
   482	
   483	
   484	def _parquet_count(path: Path) -> int:
   485	    if not path.exists():
   486	        return 0
   487	    return int(pl.scan_parquet(path).select(pl.len().alias("n")).collect().item())
   488	
   489	
   490	def _duplicate_key_count(path: Path, keys: list[str]) -> int:
   491	    if not path.exists():
   492	        return 0
   493	    return int(
   494	        pl.scan_parquet(path)
   495	        .group_by(keys)
   496	        .len()
   497	        .filter(pl.col("len") > 1)
   498	        .select((pl.col("len") - 1).sum().fill_null(0).alias("n"))
   499	        .collect()
   500	        .item()
   501	    )
   502	
   503	
   504	def validate_execution_ready_dataset(execution_ready_dir: Path) -> ExecutionReadyGateResult:
   505	    issues: list[GateIssue] = []
   506	    execution_ready_dir = execution_ready_dir.expanduser()
   507	    for filename in EXECUTION_READY_ARTIFACTS:
   508	        path = execution_ready_dir / filename
   509	        if not path.exists():
   510	            _issue(issues, "hard_fail", "missing_artifact", f"missing required artifact: {path}")
   511	
   512	    stock_path = execution_ready_dir / "daily_stock_execution.parquet"
   513	    etf_path = execution_ready_dir / "daily_etf_execution.parquet"
   514	    calendar_path = execution_ready_dir / "rebalance_calendar.parquet"
   515	    manifest_path = execution_ready_dir / "execution_manifest.json"
   516	    stock_rows = _parquet_count(stock_path)
   517	    etf_rows = _parquet_count(etf_path)
   518	    calendar_rows = _parquet_count(calendar_path)
   519	    duplicate_stock_keys = _duplicate_key_count(stock_path, ["ts_code", "trade_date"])
   520	    duplicate_etf_keys = _duplicate_key_count(etf_path, ["ts_code", "trade_date"])

docs/57_preprocessing_policy_decisions.md:16:   - The 10 `add_stock_master_row` names are April 2026 IPOs. They are present in the promoted daily panel through `20260424`, but not in the monthly universe because the latest monthly signal date is `20260331`; adding them to monthly training before the April month-end would change the monthly cadence.
docs/57_preprocessing_policy_decisions.md:40:   - Official PBOC/NBS/CSRC/SSE/SZSE/MOF/STA/State Council/NHC/USTR/MOFCOM events are accepted as free manual anchors, but each event must carry `event_ts`, `known_at_ts`, `effective_ts`, source, and URL fields. The seed anchors now use exact official pages where found, including State Council/gov.cn, CSRC, SSE, USTR, NHC, and MOF pages.
docs/57_preprocessing_policy_decisions.md:41:   - NBS release calendars and official release pages are valid timing anchors for CPI/PPI/PMI; actual values may be loaded, but historical consensus surprise must remain null without a licensed forecast source.
docs/57_preprocessing_policy_decisions.md:48:   - The local raw fundamental scope currently has all four expected datasets through `20260424`.

  1902	    features = pd.DataFrame(
  1903	        {

  1906	            "raw_value": [1.0, 2.0],
  1907	        }
  1908	    )
  1909	    if leakage_column:
  1910	        features["entry_open"] = [10.0, 20.0]
  1911	    labels = pd.DataFrame(
  1912	        {
  1913	            "ts_code": ["000001.SZ", "000002.SZ"],
  1914	            "signal_date": ["20240131", "20240131"],
  1915	            "executable_return": [0.01, -0.01],
  1916	        }
  1917	    )
  1918	    splits = pd.DataFrame({"signal_date": ["20240131"], "split": ["train"], "fold_id": [0]})
  1919	    features.to_parquet(ml_dir / "features.parquet", index=False)
  1920	    labels.to_parquet(ml_dir / "labels.parquet", index=False)
  1921	    splits.to_parquet(ml_dir / "splits.parquet", index=False)

  1923	        ml_dir / "event_windows.parquet",
  1924	        index=False,
  1925	    )
  1926	    pd.DataFrame({"column": ["entry_open"], "policy_action": ["dropped_from_features"]}).to_csv(
  1927	        ml_dir / "feature_leakage_audit.csv",
  1928	        index=False,
  1929	    )
  1930	    for name in ("preprocess_audit.csv", "feature_coverage.csv", "trial_ledger.csv"):
  1931	        pd.DataFrame({"status": ["ok"]}).to_csv(ml_dir / name, index=False)
  1932	    pd.DataFrame({"ts_code": ["000001.SZ"], "signal_date": ["20240131"], "sample_uniqueness": [1.0]}).to_parquet(
  1933	        ml_dir / "sample_uniqueness.parquet",
  1934	        index=False,
  1935	    )
  1936	    pd.DataFrame(
  1937	        {

  1949	        index=False,
  1950	    )
  1951	    pd.DataFrame({"query_id": pd.Series(dtype=str), "event_id": pd.Series(dtype=str)}).to_parquet(

  1970	    input_paths = {}

    12	def main() -> int:
    13	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Build PIT macro/event diagnostics from official anchors, regimes, and free risk series.")
    14	    parser.add_argument("--daily-regimes", default="output/data/regime_tags_daily_a_share_20080101_20260424.csv")
    15	    parser.add_argument("--monthly-regimes", default="output/data/regime_tags_monthly_a_share_20080101_20260424.csv")
    16	    parser.add_argument("--official-events", default="sources/macro_events/official_event_seed.csv")
    17	    parser.add_argument("--gpr-monthly", default="sources/macro_events/gpr/data_gpr_export.xls")
    18	    parser.add_argument("--gpr-daily", default="sources/macro_events/gpr/data_gpr_daily_recent.xls")
    19	    parser.add_argument("--gdelt-query-specs", default="sources/macro_events/gdelt/gdelt_doc_api_seed_queries.csv")
    20	    parser.add_argument(
    21	        "--enable-gdelt-doc-api",
    22	        action="store_true",
    23	        help="Fetch timestamped GDELT DOC API article-list diagnostics. Failures are audited but not hard failures.",
    24	    )
    25	    parser.add_argument("--gdelt-timeout-seconds", type=int, default=60)
    26	    parser.add_argument("--gdelt-delay-seconds", type=float, default=3.0)
    27	    parser.add_argument("--gdelt-max-attempts", type=int, default=2)
    28	    parser.add_argument("--output-label", default="a_share_all_v1_full_20260424")
    29	    args = parser.parse_args()
    30	
    31	    settings = load_settings(require_token=False)
    32	    daily_regimes = pd.read_csv(resolve_project_path(settings.project_root, args.daily_regimes))
    33	    monthly_regimes = pd.read_csv(resolve_project_path(settings.project_root, args.monthly_regimes))
    34	    official_events_path = resolve_project_path(settings.project_root, args.official_events)
    35	    official_events = pd.read_csv(official_events_path, dtype=str)
    36	    gdelt_query_specs_path = resolve_project_path(settings.project_root, args.gdelt_query_specs)
    37	    gdelt_query_specs = pd.read_csv(gdelt_query_specs_path, dtype=str) if gdelt_query_specs_path.exists() else pd.DataFrame()
    38	    artifacts = build_macro_event_artifacts(
    39	        daily_regimes=daily_regimes,
    40	        monthly_regimes=monthly_regimes,
    41	        official_events=official_events,
    42	        gpr_monthly_path=resolve_project_path(settings.project_root, args.gpr_monthly),
    43	        gpr_daily_path=resolve_project_path(settings.project_root, args.gpr_daily),
    44	        gdelt_query_specs=gdelt_query_specs,
    45	        enable_gdelt_doc_api=args.enable_gdelt_doc_api,
    46	        gdelt_timeout_seconds=args.gdelt_timeout_seconds,
    47	        gdelt_delay_seconds=args.gdelt_delay_seconds,
    48	        gdelt_max_attempts=args.gdelt_max_attempts,
    49	    )
    50	    output_dir = settings.curated_root / "ml_ready_v1" / args.output_label
    51	    paths = write_macro_event_artifacts(artifacts, output_dir)
    52	    payload = {
    53	        "status": artifacts.quality_report["status"],
    54	        "output_dir": str(output_dir),
    55	        "official_events": str(official_events_path),
    56	        "gdelt_query_specs": str(gdelt_query_specs_path),
    57	        "gdelt_doc_api_enabled": args.enable_gdelt_doc_api,
    58	        "paths": paths,
    59	        "quality_report": artifacts.quality_report,
    60	    }
    61	    print(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2, default=str))
    62	    return 0 if payload["status"] == "completed" else 1
    63	
    64	
    65	if __name__ == "__main__":
    66	    raise SystemExit(main())

   167	def normalize_official_events(frame: pd.DataFrame) -> tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame]:
   168	    out = frame.copy()
   169	    for col in OFFICIAL_EVENT_COLUMNS:
   170	        if col not in out.columns:
   171	            out[col] = ""
   172	    for col in ("event_ts", "known_at_ts", "effective_ts"):
   173	        out[col] = out[col].fillna("").astype(str).str[:8]
   174	    out["feature_policy"] = out["feature_policy"].fillna("").replace("", "diagnostics_only")
   175	    out["reliability_tier"] = out["reliability_tier"].fillna("").replace("", "official")
   176	    issues = []
   177	    for row in out.itertuples(index=False):
   178	        event_id = str(getattr(row, "event_id"))
   179	        known_at = str(getattr(row, "known_at_ts"))
   180	        event_ts = str(getattr(row, "event_ts"))
   181	        source_url = str(getattr(row, "source_url", ""))
   182	        surprise_value = str(getattr(row, "surprise_value", ""))
   183	        forecast_value = str(getattr(row, "forecast_value", ""))
   184	        surprise_source = str(getattr(row, "surprise_source", ""))
   185	        if not known_at or known_at == "nan":
   186	            issues.append({"severity": "hard_fail", "event_id": event_id, "issue": "known_at_ts_missing"})
   187	        if not event_ts or event_ts == "nan":
   188	            issues.append({"severity": "hard_fail", "event_id": event_id, "issue": "event_ts_missing"})
   189	        if not source_url or source_url == "nan":
   190	            issues.append({"severity": "warn", "event_id": event_id, "issue": "source_url_missing"})
   191	        if surprise_value not in {"", "nan"} and (forecast_value in {"", "nan"} or surprise_source in {"", "nan"}):
   192	            issues.append({"severity": "hard_fail", "event_id": event_id, "issue": "surprise_without_forecast_or_source"})
   193	    normalized = normalize_event_frame(
   194	        out.rename(columns={"source_url": "source_detail_url"}),
   195	        default_source="official_manual_events",
   196	    )
   197	    for col in ("feature_policy", "actual_value", "forecast_value", "surprise_value", "surprise_source", "notes", "source_detail_url"):
   198	        if col not in normalized.columns:
   199	            normalized[col] = ""
   200	    return normalized, pd.DataFrame(issues)

   203	def regime_tags_to_events(daily_regimes: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
   204	    daily = daily_regimes.copy()
   205	    if daily.empty:
   206	        return pd.DataFrame()
   207	    daily["trade_date"] = daily["trade_date"].astype(str)
   208	    event_type = daily["regime_type"].astype(str) if "regime_type" in daily.columns else pd.Series("regime_tag", index=daily.index)
   209	    regime_id = daily["regime_id"] if "regime_id" in daily.columns else pd.Series("", index=daily.index)
   210	    regime_label = daily["regime_label"] if "regime_label" in daily.columns else pd.Series("", index=daily.index)
   211	    regime_notes = daily["regime_notes"] if "regime_notes" in daily.columns else pd.Series("", index=daily.index)
   212	    rows = pd.DataFrame(
   213	        {
   214	            "event_id": "regime_daily_" + daily["trade_date"],
   215	            "event_family": "regime",
   216	            "event_type": event_type,
   217	            "scope_type": "market",
   218	            "scope_id": "A_SHARE",
   219	            "event_ts": daily["trade_date"],
   220	            "known_at_ts": daily["trade_date"],
   221	            "effective_ts": daily["trade_date"],
   222	            "source": "internal_regime_tags",
   223	            "source_detail_url": "",
   224	            "reliability_tier": "internal_diagnostic",
   225	            "feature_policy": "diagnostics_only",
   226	            "regime_id": regime_id,
   227	            "regime_label": regime_label,
   228	            "regime_notes": regime_notes,
   229	        }
   230	    )
   231	    return normalize_event_frame(rows, default_source="internal_regime_tags")

1	# Deprecation Inventory
     2	
     3	Date: 2026-04-27
     4	
     5	## Current Default
     6	
     7	The approved preprocessing path is:
     8	
     9	```bash
    10	uv run quant-build-production-dataset
    11	uv run quant-gate-ml-dataset --ml-ready-dir data_lake/curated/ml_ready_v1/a_share_all_v1_full_20260424
    12	```
    13	
    14	Production-style factor and strategy work must consume:
    15	
    16	```text
    17	data_lake/curated/ml_ready_v1/a_share_all_v1_full_20260424/features.parquet
    18	data_lake/curated/ml_ready_v1/a_share_all_v1_full_20260424/labels.parquet
    19	data_lake/curated/ml_ready_v1/a_share_all_v1_full_20260424/splits.parquet
    20	```
    21	
    22	Raw daily panels, raw monthly histories, and generated research CSVs are diagnostic or historical inputs unless a command explicitly gates them back into the ML-ready artifact.
    23	
    24	## Deprecated CLI Entrypoints
    25	
    26	These commands are retained for historical replication only and require an explicit bypass flag:
    27	
    28	| Command                         | Status                                                                                                | Replacement                                                              |
    29	| ------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
    30	| `quant-build-snapshot`          | Deprecated mainboard-only one-day snapshot builder. Requires `--allow-deprecated-mainboard-snapshot`. | `quant-build-production-dataset`                                         |
    31	| `quant-build-event-layer`       | Deprecated regime-only event copier. Requires `--allow-deprecated-event-layer`.                       | `quant-build-macro-event-layer` through `quant-build-production-dataset` |
    32	| `quant-run-prototype`           | Legacy raw monthly-history research bypass. Requires `--allow-research-bypass`.                       | ML-ready `features.parquet`, `labels.parquet`, `splits.parquet`          |
    33	| `quant-run-capacity-sweep`      | Legacy raw monthly-history research bypass. Requires `--allow-research-bypass`.                       | ML-ready artifact plus current execution/cost evaluator                  |
    34	| `quant-run-hybrid`              | Legacy raw monthly-history research bypass. Requires `--allow-research-bypass`.                       | ML-ready artifact plus current benchmark/ETF diagnostics                 |
    35	| `quant-run-alpha-lab`           | Legacy raw daily/monthly research bypass. Requires `--allow-research-bypass`.                         | ML-ready artifact                                                        |
    36	| `quant-run-alpha-lab-opt`       | Legacy raw daily/monthly research bypass. Requires `--allow-research-bypass`.                         | ML-ready artifact                                                        |
    37	| `quant-run-paper-alpha-lab`     | External paper-factor/raw-source research bypass. Requires `--allow-research-bypass`.                 | ML-ready artifact plus explicit factor adapter                           |
    38	| `quant-run-buildable-alpha-lab` | Legacy direct panel/fundamental input research bypass. Requires `--allow-research-bypass`.            | ML-ready artifact                                                        |
    39	
    40	`quant-validate-tushare` remains active, but its payload is now an all-A-share access check with the mainboard count marked as diagnostic.
    41	
    42	## Deprecated Scripts
    43	
    44	These top-level scripts predate the all-A-share ML-ready pipeline and now fail fast unless run with `--allow-deprecated-script`:
    45	
    46	| Script                                  | Reason                                                                       |
    47	| --------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
    48	| `scripts/build_mainboard_snapshot.py`   | Builds one-day mainboard-only CSVs outside the approved production artifact. |
    49	| `scripts/validate_tushare_mainboard.py` | Validates the old mainboard-only source scope.                               |
    50	| `scripts/screen_universe_designs.py`    | Screens artificial mainboard-era universe slices.                            |
    51	| `scripts/run_capacity_scale_exact.py`   | Hardcodes the old mainboard monthly CSV and legacy capacity stack.           |
    52	| `scripts/benchmark_polars_builds.py`    | Targets `mainboard_reduced_v4` and stale package paths.                      |
    53	
    54	`scripts/validate_backtesting_stack.py` is still a generic environment diagnostic and is not deprecated by this pass.
    55	
    56	## Historical Docs
    57	
    58	The numbered docs before the all-A-share production freeze remain useful as research history, but they are not current operating guidance when they mention mainboard-only, `mainboard_reduced_*`, diagnostic universe slices, or direct raw-panel factor runs.
    59	
    60	Current operating docs are:
    61	
    62	- `AGENTS.md`
    63	- `docs/52_all_a_share_default_dataset_freeze.md`
    64	- `docs/53_preprocessing_tasks_5_9_completion.md`
    65	- `docs/54_future_roadmap.md`
    66	- `docs/55_pit_industry_layer.md`
    67	- `docs/56_baostock_reference_layer.md`
    68	- `docs/57_preprocessing_policy_decisions.md`
    69	- `docs/58_non_l2_paper_factor_build_specs.md`
    70	- `docs/59_gdelt_legacy_backfill_decision.md`
    71	- `docs/60_deprecation_inventory.md`
    72	
    73	## Historical Generated Data
    74	
    75	Do not delete generated outputs automatically. Treat these families as historical or diagnostic unless a current command explicitly consumes them:
    76	
    77	| Path family                                   | Status                                                               |
    78	| --------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
    79	| `data_lake/curated/mainboard_reduced_*`       | Historical reduced-universe experiments.                             |
    80	| `data_lake/curated/mainboard_all_v1`          | Superseded by all-A-share production artifacts.                      |
    81	| `output/data/mainboard_*`                     | Historical mainboard-era CSV outputs.                                |
    82	| `output/research/*mainboard*`                 | Historical research outputs.                                         |
    83	| `output/research/capacity_scale_exact_*`      | Historical capacity-scale run outputs.                               |
    84	| `output/research/universe_design_*`           | Historical artificial-slice screening outputs.                       |
    85	| `output/research/polars_build_benchmark.json` | Historical migration benchmark, superseded by current quality gates. |
    86	
    87	Storage cleanup is a separate destructive operation. Archive or delete only after confirming that no current manifest, test, or doc link still depends on the file.
    88	
    89	## Deprecation Rule
    90	
    91	New preprocessing work should not add another raw-panel entrypoint. If a raw source must be exposed for diagnostics, the command must:
    92	
    93	- Say it is a research bypass in help/docs.
    94	- Require an explicit bypass flag.
    95	- Point to the ML-ready production artifact and `quant-gate-ml-dataset`.
    96	- Avoid writing into the active ML-ready directory unless it passes the production gate.

    11	from quantresearch.core.config import Settings
    12	
    13	
    14	@dataclass(frozen=True)
    15	class DataLakeLayout:
    16	    root: Path
    17	    raw_root: Path
    18	    curated_root: Path
    19	    marts_root: Path
    20	    manifest_db: Path
    21	
    22	
    23	def create_layout(settings: Settings) -> DataLakeLayout:
    24	    settings.data_lake_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    25	    settings.raw_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    26	    settings.curated_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    27	    settings.marts_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    28	    return DataLakeLayout(
    29	        root=settings.data_lake_root,
    30	        raw_root=settings.raw_root,
    31	        curated_root=settings.curated_root,
    32	        marts_root=settings.marts_root,
    33	        manifest_db=settings.marts_root / "research.duckdb",
    34	    )
    35	
    36	
    37	def connect_manifest(layout: DataLakeLayout) -> duckdb.DuckDBPyConnection:
    38	    conn = duckdb.connect(str(layout.manifest_db))
    39	    conn.execute(
    40	        """
    41	        create table if not exists dataset_files (
    42	            dataset_name varchar,
    43	            scope_name varchar,
    44	            start_date varchar,
    45	            end_date varchar,
    46	            partition_year integer,
    47	            partition_month integer,
    48	            row_count bigint,
    49	            column_count integer,
    50	            path varchar primary key,
    51	            schema_json varchar,
    52	            written_at timestamp default current_timestamp
    53	        )
    54	        """
    55	    )
    56	    conn.execute(
    57	        """
    58	        create table if not exists backfill_runs (
    59	            run_id varchar primary key,
    60	            scope_name varchar,
    61	            start_date varchar,
    62	            end_date varchar,
    63	            status varchar,
    64	            notes varchar,
    65	            created_at timestamp default current_timestamp,
    66	            completed_at timestamp
    67	        )
    68	        """
    69	    )
    70	    conn.execute(
    71	        """
    72	        create table if not exists backfill_run_datasets (
    73	            run_id varchar,
    74	            dataset_name varchar,
    75	            path varchar,
    76	            row_count bigint,
    77	            foreign key(run_id) references backfill_runs(run_id)
    78	        )
    79	        """
    80	    )
    81	    return conn
    82	
    83	
    84	def start_run(conn: duckdb.DuckDBPyConnection, scope_name: str, start_date: str, end_date: str, notes: str = "") -> str:
    85	    run_id = uuid.uuid4().hex
    86	    conn.execute(
    87	        """
    88	        insert into backfill_runs(run_id, scope_name, start_date, end_date, status, notes)
    89	        values (?, ?, ?, ?, 'running', ?)
    90	        """,
    91	        [run_id, scope_name, start_date, end_date, notes],
    92	    )
    93	    return run_id
    94	
    95	
    96	def finish_run(conn: duckdb.DuckDBPyConnection, run_id: str, status: str, notes: str = "") -> None:
    97	    conn.execute(
    98	        """
    99	        update backfill_runs
   100	        set status = ?, notes = coalesce(nullif(?, ''), notes), completed_at = current_timestamp
   101	        where run_id = ?
   102	        """,
   103	        [status, notes, run_id],
   104	    )
   105	
   106	
   107	def register_dataset_file(
   108	    conn: duckdb.DuckDBPyConnection,
   109	    run_id: str,
   110	    dataset_name: str,
   111	    scope_name: str,
   112	    start_date: str,
   113	    end_date: str,
   114	    partition_year: int,
   115	    partition_month: int,
   116	    path: Path,
   117	    frame: pd.DataFrame,
   118	) -> None:
   119	    schema_json = json.dumps({col: str(dtype) for col, dtype in frame.dtypes.items()}, ensure_ascii=False)
   120	    conn.execute("delete from dataset_files where path = ?", [str(path)])
   121	    conn.execute(
   122	        """
   123	        insert into dataset_files(
   124	            dataset_name, scope_name, start_date, end_date, partition_year, partition_month,
   125	            row_count, column_count, path, schema_json
   126	        )
   127	        values (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   128	        """,
   129	        [
   130	            dataset_name,
   131	            scope_name,
   132	            start_date,
   133	            end_date,
   134	            partition_year,
   135	            partition_month,
   136	            int(len(frame)),
   137	            int(len(frame.columns)),
   138	            str(path),
   139	            schema_json,
   140	        ],
   141	    )
   142	    conn.execute(
   143	        """
   144	        insert into backfill_run_datasets(run_id, dataset_name, path, row_count)
   145	        values (?, ?, ?, ?)
   146	        """,
   147	        [run_id, dataset_name, str(path), int(len(frame))],
   148	    )
   149	
   150	
   151	def write_parquet(frame: pd.DataFrame, path: Path) -> None:
   152	    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   153	    frame.to_parquet(path, index=False)

item.aggregated_output:
AGENTS.md:144:The full ETF reference backfill is date-mode and checkpointed. It writes Tushare `fund_adj`, `fund_nav`, and derived close-to-NAV premium/discount for every code in the local ETF daily panel. Use the older proxy-only mode only for benchmark proxy diagnostics:
docs/62_time_series_foundation_models_for_stock_etf_quant_research.md:22:4. FinText-TSFM checkpoints for finance-pretrained TimesFM-style return models, after data-lineage and cutoff checks.
docs/62_time_series_foundation_models_for_stock_etf_quant_research.md:90:The Hugging Face model card for `google/timesfm-2.5-200m-pytorch` lists Apache-2.0 licensing and says the checkpoint is pretrained using GiftEvalPretrain, Wikimedia Pageviews with a November 2023 cutoff, Google Trends top queries with an end-of-2022 cutoff, and synthetic/augmented data. Source: <https://huggingface.co/google/timesfm-2.5-200m-pytorch>
docs/62_time_series_foundation_models_for_stock_etf_quant_research.md:128:The Kronos repo describes it as an open-source foundation model for financial candlesticks trained on data from more than 45 global exchanges. It releases Kronos-mini, Kronos-small, and Kronos-base as open-source checkpoints, while Kronos-large is not open-source in the model zoo. The repo license is MIT. Source: <https://github.com/shiyu-coder/Kronos>
docs/62_time_series_foundation_models_for_stock_etf_quant_research.md:142:The Hugging Face card for `FinText/TimesFM_20M_2016_Global` says the model is a 19.8M parameter TimesFM architecture checkpoint pretrained on global financial excess returns from 1990 to 2016, under Apache-2.0. The 2023 checkpoint says the analogous global dataset spans 1990 to 2023. Sources: <https://huggingface.co/FinText/TimesFM_20M_2016_Global>, <https://huggingface.co/FinText/TimesFM_20M_2023_Global>
docs/62_time_series_foundation_models_for_stock_etf_quant_research.md:147:- Year-specific checkpoints are useful for cutoff-controlled experiments.
docs/62_time_series_foundation_models_for_stock_etf_quant_research.md:148:- The 2016 checkpoint is safer for evaluating 2017+ than the 2023 checkpoint.
docs/62_time_series_foundation_models_for_stock_etf_quant_research.md:299:- model metadata: checkpoint, license, context length, horizon, target transform, cutoff assumption.
docs/62_time_series_foundation_models_for_stock_etf_quant_research.md:344:- Use finance-pretrained checkpoints before generic checkpoints when cutoff-safe.
docs/62_time_series_foundation_models_for_stock_etf_quant_research.md:348:- Record exact checkpoint hashes and training windows.
docs/62_time_series_foundation_models_for_stock_etf_quant_research.md:397:- Do not use any checkpoint whose pretraining cutoff overlaps the evaluation window unless the experiment is explicitly labeled in-sample/diagnostic.
docs/62_time_series_foundation_models_for_stock_etf_quant_research.md:398:- Do not use a global finance checkpoint without checking whether A-share/China/ETF data or proxy instruments are included.
docs/62_time_series_foundation_models_for_stock_etf_quant_research.md:415:| 1 | Chronos-2 | covariate/multivariate probabilistic forecasts | open repo/checkpoints | daily/weekly return, vol, liquidity features |
docs/62_time_series_foundation_models_for_stock_etf_quant_research.md:416:| 2 | TimesFM 2.5 | long-context univariate/XReg quantile forecasts | Apache-2.0 checkpoint | horizon features and uncertainty ranks |
docs/62_time_series_foundation_models_for_stock_etf_quant_research.md:418:| 4 | FinText-TSFM | finance-pretrained return models | HF checkpoints, Apache-2.0 cards | cutoff-safe global return baseline |

    25	def _glob_path(layout: DataLakeLayout, scope_name: str, dataset_name: str) -> str:
    26	    return str(layout.raw_root / scope_name / dataset_name / "**" / "*.parquet")
    27	
    28	
    29	def _read_parquet_view(conn: duckdb.DuckDBPyConnection, view_name: str, layout: DataLakeLayout, scope_name: str, dataset_name: str) -> None:
    30	    conn.read_parquet(_glob_path(layout, scope_name, dataset_name), union_by_name=True).create_view(view_name, replace=True)
    31

    33	def _match_series(left: pd.Series, right: pd.Series, tolerance: float = 1e-9) -> pd.Series:
    34	    left_num = pd.to_numeric(left, errors="coerce")
    35	    right_num = pd.to_numeric(right, errors="coerce")
    36	    both_missing = left_num.isna() & right_num.isna()
    37	    return both_missing | ((left_num - right_num).abs() <= tolerance)

    40	def _build_monthly_crosscheck(monthly: pd.DataFrame, panel: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    41	    monthly_view = monthly.copy()
    42	    monthly_view["signal_date"] = monthly_view["signal_date"].astype(str)
    43	    monthly_view["rebalance_date"] = monthly_view["rebalance_date"].astype(str)
    44	    panel_view = panel.copy()
    45	    panel_view["trade_date"] = panel_view["trade_date"].astype(str)

    47	    signal_pairs = {

    49	        "signal_amount": "amount",

    52	        "signal_up_limit": "up_limit",
    53	        "signal_down_limit": "down_limit",
    54	        "net_mf_amount": "net_mf_amount",
    55	        "adj_factor": "adj_factor",
    56	        "is_st_name": "is_st_name",
    57	        "is_suspended_signal": "is_suspended",

   221	        """
   222	        select dataset_name, count(*) as files, sum(row_count) as rows
   223	        from dataset_files
   224	        where scope_name = ?
   225	        group by 1
   226	        order by 1
   227	        """,
   228	        [scope_name],
   229	    ).fetch_df()
   230	
   231	    trade_cal = conn.execute(
   232	        """
   233	        select cal_date

   235	        where is_open = 1
   236	        order by cal_date
   237	        """,
   238	        [_glob_path(layout, scope_name, "trade_cal")],
   239	    ).fetch_df()
   240	    open_days = set(trade_cal["cal_date"].astype(str))
   241	
   242	    missing_rows: list[dict[str, str | int]] = []
   243	    for dataset_name in ("daily", "daily_basic", "moneyflow", "stk_limit", "adj_factor"):
   244	        frame = conn.execute(
   245	            """
   246	            select trade_date, count(*) as rows
   247	            from read_parquet(?, union_by_name=true)
   248	            group by 1
   249	            order by 1
   250	            """,
   251	            [_glob_path(layout, scope_name, dataset_name)],
   252	        ).fetch_df()
   253	        present = set(frame["trade_date"].astype(str))
   254	        for trade_date in sorted(open_days - present):
   255	            missing_rows.append({"dataset_name": dataset_name, "trade_date": trade_date})
   256	    missing_dates = pd.DataFrame(missing_rows)
   257	
   258	    duplicate_rows: list[dict[str, str | int]] = []
   259	    for dataset_name in ("daily", "daily_basic", "moneyflow", "stk_limit", "adj_factor"):
   260	        dup_df = conn.execute(
   261	            """
   262	            select ts_code, trade_date, count(*) as dup_count
   263	            from read_parquet(?, union_by_name=true)
   264	            group by 1,2
   265	            having count(*) > 1
   266	            order by dup_count desc, trade_date, ts_code
   267	            """,
   268	            [_glob_path(layout, scope_name, dataset_name)],
   269	        ).fetch_df()
   270	        if not dup_df.empty:
   271	            dup_df.insert(0, "dataset_name", dataset_name)
   272	            duplicate_rows.append(dup_df)
   273	    duplicate_keys = (
   274	        pd.concat(duplicate_rows, ignore_index=True)
   275	        if duplicate_rows
   276	        else pd.DataFrame(columns=["dataset_name", "ts_code", "trade_date", "dup_count"])
   277	    )
   278	
   279	    curated_daily_panel_path = build_curated_daily_panel(
   280	        layout=layout,
   281	        scope_name=scope_name,
   282	        stock_master=stock_master,
   283	        extra_ts_codes=extra_ts_codes,
   284	    )
   285	    panel = pd.read_parquet(curated_daily_panel_path)
   286	    monthly = pd.read_csv(monthly_history_with_universes_csv)
   287	    panel["trade_date"] = panel["trade_date"].astype(str)

   289	    monthly["rebalance_date"] = monthly["rebalance_date"].astype(str)
   290	    sample_crosscheck = _build_monthly_crosscheck(monthly=monthly, panel=panel)
   291	
   292	    conn.close()
   293	    return IntegrityArtifacts(
   294	        summary=summary,
   295	        missing_dates=missing_dates,
   296	        duplicate_keys=duplicate_keys,
   297	        sample_crosscheck=sample_crosscheck,
   298	        curated_daily_panel_path=curated_daily_panel_path,
   299	    )

   108	def month_chunks(start_date: str, end_date: str) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
   109	    starts = pd.date_range(
   110	        pd.Timestamp(start_date),
   111	        pd.Timestamp(end_date),
   112	        freq="MS",
   113	    )
   114	    if not starts.empty and starts[0] > pd.Timestamp(start_date):
   115	        starts = starts.insert(0, pd.Timestamp(start_date))
   116	    chunks: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
   117	    current = pd.Timestamp(start_date)
   118	    while current <= pd.Timestamp(end_date):
   119	        month_end = min(current + pd.offsets.MonthEnd(0), pd.Timestamp(end_date))
   120	        chunks.append((current.strftime("%Y%m%d"), month_end.strftime("%Y%m%d")))
   121	        current = month_end + pd.Timedelta(days=1)
   122	    return chunks
   123	
   124	
   125	def year_chunks(start_date: str, end_date: str) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
   126	    start = pd.Timestamp(start_date)
   127	    end = pd.Timestamp(end_date)
   128	    chunks: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
   129	    current = start
   130	    while current <= end:
   131	        year_end = min(pd.Timestamp(year=current.year, month=12, day=31), end)
   132	        chunks.append((current.strftime("%Y%m%d"), year_end.strftime("%Y%m%d")))
   133	        current = year_end + pd.Timedelta(days=1)
   134	    return chunks

{"summary":"Formal read-only review completed. The plan is directionally sound, but it needs tighter sequencing, output-contract details, and migration safeguards before it is execution-ready.","markdown_response":"**Formal Review**

I inspected `/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md` and the relevant local implementation under `/Users/roy/Documents/quant`. I also verified the 2026 Labor Day calendar claim against the official SSE closure page: SSE lists May 1-5, 2026 closed and May 6 reopening, so the `today=2026-05-02 -> daily_end_date=20260430` fixture is well grounded. Source: https://www.sse.com.cn/disclosure/dealinstruc/closed/

Findings are ordered by severity.

1. **High: Freshness/context work is sequenced before the shared step contract it depends on**

The plan starts with date/context resolution, dry-run schema, freshness registry, and checkpoint metadata, then extracts shared callable step APIs in step 2. That risks building a parallel metadata system that later has to be reconciled with the actual step APIs. Current endpoint/date metadata is scattered across `data/backfill.py`, `data/integrity.py`, individual CLIs, and preprocessing builders, so duplicating family inputs/outputs/freshness first is likely to drift.

Recommendation: make the first milestone a minimal `StepSpec`/`FamilySpec` registry with stable step IDs, explicit inputs/outputs, mode action, freshness policy, checkpoint version, and callable placeholder. Build resolver, dry-run, checkpoint, and execution from that one registry.

2. **High: Manual CLI guard rollout can break non-production callers not covered by the sequence**

The sequence only gates direct CLIs after production orchestration is self-contained, but those commands are still documented and exposed as normal operator commands. For example, `quant-build-execution-ready-dataset` is a public console script in `pyproject.toml` and is documented in `AGENTS.md`; its CLI currently has no bypass flag. Other current docs and active CLIs still rely on direct build/backfill commands and `20260424` defaults.

Recommendation: add an explicit caller inventory before adding guards: docs, tests, shell snippets, research CLIs, scheduled jobs, and console-script references. Add a CI assertion that guarded CLIs are referenced only as manual bypass examples or through the production orchestrator. Consider a warning-only transition for one release unless this is strictly internal.

3. **High: ML cutoff requirements need to say transforms, splits, and uniqueness are recomputed after filtering**

The plan correctly says incomplete latest-month rows must not appear in gated ML training artifacts. Current assembly builds labels and splits over all `base["signal_date"]`, then writes `features.parquet`, `labels.parquet`, and `splits.parquet`. If the implementation only filters labels or relies on `exclude_from_label`, incomplete rows can still affect split assignment, transform fitting, feature coverage, sample uniqueness, and manifests.

Recommendation: make the plan explicit: derive `training_base = base[signal_date <= ml_training_label_end]` before label build, split build, transform fit, sample uniqueness, feature coverage, and manifest row counts. Build prediction-only artifacts from a separate `prediction_base` path that never flows through `quant-gate-ml-dataset`.

4. **High: `--allow-manual-data-step` explicit-argument enforcement is underspecified**

The plan requires bypass runs to use “real arguments provided explicitly,” but normal `argparse` output cannot distinguish a user-supplied value from a default value when the values match. This matters because the guarded CLIs currently have many production-sensitive defaults, including ML inputs, execution-ready inputs, benchmark paths, ETF references, and `20260424` labels.

Recommendation: require the central helper to inspect raw `argv`, use `argparse.SUPPRESS`/sentinel defaults, or wrap parser registration so each required option records whether it was explicitly supplied. Tests should include “same as default but explicitly supplied” and “implicit default” cases.

5. **Medium: Execution-ready lineage change needs a concrete API and gate update**

The plan says execution-ready artifacts must not treat the skipped current ML-ready label as an input, but the current builder takes `--ml-ready-dir` by default and the manifest source boundary says `curated_daily_panels_plus_gated_monthly_ml_ready`. The plan does not specify whether `ml_ready_dir` is removed, renamed to `training_context_ml_ready_dir`, optionalized, or validated separately.

Recommendation: add a specific migration item for `build_execution_manifest` and `cli_build_execution_ready_dataset`: execution inputs should be daily stock/ETF/reference artifacts; historical ML lineage should be a separate optional manifest field with its own hash/status and no execution gate dependency.

6. **Medium: GDELT optionality conflicts with the current ML gate artifact contract**

The plan makes GDELT DOC diagnostics optional by default, but the current ML-ready production gate requires `gdelt_doc_articles.parquet` and `gdelt_extract_audit.csv`. If “skipped optional” means files are absent, daily/monthly runs will fail the gate; if it means empty audit artifacts are still written, the plan should say so.

Recommendation: define the contract: either always materialize empty `not_enabled` GDELT audit artifacts for gated ML outputs, or update `quant-gate-ml-dataset` to accept an explicit skipped status in the manifest.

7. **Medium: Endpoint freshness needs completeness semantics, not only max-date checks**

The plan asks freshness probes to cover daily, `daily_basic`, moneyflow, limit data, ETF daily, benchmark data, fund NAV/adj, fundamentals, and more. Current ingestion sometimes treats empty endpoint returns specially, such as synthesizing empty moneyflow rows when the endpoint returns empty for a date with daily codes. Sparse endpoints and no-data days can be valid, stale, permission-limited, or incomplete depending on endpoint and universe.

Recommendation: for each freshness family, define expected key coverage, acceptable empty responses, row-count lower bounds, and fallback behavior. Keep local manifest/raw-file checks first, and use live Tushare probes only where local evidence cannot prove freshness, to reduce quota/flakiness.

8. **Medium: Checkpoint/resume lacks atomicity and concurrency safeguards**

The plan adds step statuses and hash-based resume decisions, but current builders write directly to final paths. Without temp output directories, atomic rename, run locks, and failed-run isolation, resume can reuse partially written artifacts or two operators can corrupt the same label.

Recommendation: add checkpoint lifecycle rules: per-run working directory, lock file, temp outputs, atomic promotion, failed-output quarantine, and no resume reuse unless the previous step reached a terminal `completed/gated/reused_existing` state with matching hashes.

9. **Medium: Calendar source acquisition and fallback are missing**

The SSE holiday fixture is correct, but the plan depends on the official SSE calendar as the base calendar without saying how it is fetched, parsed, cached, versioned, or overridden when the website changes or is unavailable. Since date resolution controls live data writes, this should not rely on an unversioned scrape path.

Recommendation: add a pinned exchange-calendar artifact with source URL, retrieved timestamp, parser version, hash, and manual override process. Use Tushare `trade_cal` as cross-check as planned, but make resolver behavior deterministic when the official source cannot be refreshed.

10. **Low: Mode matrix mixes actions with dependency/blocking modifiers**

The valid action list is singular, but matrix cells use combinations such as `execute and block_if_stale`, `probe plus reuse_existing`, and `reuse_existing or block_if_stale`. That will make dry-run schema and tests ambiguous.

Recommendation: split each family row into `action`, `freshness_check`, `blocking_policy`, and `consumers`, or define a typed list of actions. Then dry-run JSON can be validated strictly.

11. **Low: Active artifact semantics remain unclear**

The plan says canonical research-run pointers are not updated automatically, while the unified command generates new labels and paths. Without an active-pointer policy, operators may produce fresh gated artifacts that downstream research commands still ignore because many defaults still point to `a_share_all_v1_full_20260424`.

Recommendation: add an explicit non-goal plus operator output: “fresh artifact created but not promoted,” with next commands to gate/promote/use it. Or add a separate manual `promote-active-artifact` step outside this plan.

Overall: the plan is strong on desired behavior and test intent. The main improvement is to make the shared step/family contract the first implementation artifact, then drive resolver, dry-run, execution, checkpointing, and deprecation from that contract. That reduces duplication and makes the broad migration safer.","findings":[{"id":"F1","severity":"high","confidence":0.88,"title":"Freshness/context work is sequenced before the shared step contract it depends on","location":"/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md Implementation Sequence step 1; src/quantresearch/data/backfill.py:21; src/quantresearch/data/integrity.py:231; src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_production_dataset.py:71","evidence":"The plan adds date/context resolution, dry-run schema, freshness registry, and checkpoint metadata before extracting shared callable step APIs. Current metadata is scattered: DATE_FIELDS in backfill, integrity checks in data/integrity.py, and orchestration commands in cli_build_production_dataset.py.","recommendation":"Create a StepSpec/FamilySpec registry first, then derive resolver, dry-run, checkpoint, and execution from it.","actionable":true},{"id":"F2","severity":"high","confidence":0.9,"title":"Manual CLI guard rollout can break non-production callers not covered by the sequence","location":"/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md Legacy CLI Deprecation; pyproject.toml:57; AGENTS.md:65; src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_execution_ready_dataset.py:74","evidence":"The plan gates direct CLIs after production orchestration is migrated, but direct build commands are still public/documented. `quant-build-execution-ready-dataset` is exposed in pyproject and documented as a normal build path, with no bypass flag today.","recommendation":"Add a pre-guard caller inventory and CI check for docs/tests/scripts/schedulers. Migrate or explicitly mark every remaining direct invocation before hard-failing.","actionable":true},{"id":"F3","severity":"high","confidence":0.86,"title":"ML cutoff requirements need post-filter recomputation of transforms, splits, and uniqueness","location":"/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md Refresh Context; src/quantresearch/preprocessing/ml_dataset.py:444; src/quantresearch/preprocessing/ml_dataset.py:445; src/quantresearch/preprocessing/ml_dataset.py:472; src/quantresearch/preprocessing/ml_dataset.py:546","evidence":"Current assembly builds labels and splits over the full base signal-date set, fits transforms from feature splits, then writes training artifacts. Filtering only labels or relying on exclude flags would still let incomplete dates affect features/splits/transforms/manifests.","recommendation":"State that `training_base` is cut to `signal_date <= ml_training_label_end` before label build, split build, transform fit, sample uniqueness, coverage, and writes. Prediction-only rows must use a separate pipeline/output boundary.","actionable":true},{"id":"F4","severity":"high","confidence":0.82,"title":"Explicit bypass argument enforcement is underspecified","location":"/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md Legacy CLI Deprecation; src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_ml_dataset.py:35; src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_execution_ready_dataset.py:76; src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_fundamental_features.py:15","evidence":"The plan requires production-sensitive defaults to be forbidden in bypass mode unless arguments are explicit, but argparse normally loses whether a value came from the user or from a default. Many guarded CLIs currently have sensitive defaults.","recommendation":"Require raw argv/sentinel tracking in the central helper and add tests for implicit default, explicit same-as-default, and explicit custom values.","actionable":true},{"id":"F5","severity":"medium","confidence":0.84,"title":"Execution-ready lineage change needs a concrete API and gate update","location":"/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md Refresh Context; src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_execution_ready_dataset.py:76; src/quantresearch/preprocessing/execution_ready.py:461","evidence":"The plan says skipped current ML-ready labels must not be execution inputs, but the current builder accepts `--ml-ready-dir` and the manifest source boundary says `curated_daily_panels_plus_gated_monthly_ml_ready`.","recommendation":"Specify the migration: remove or rename `ml_ready_dir` to optional `training_context_ml_ready_dir`, record it separately, and update execution manifest/gate expectations.","actionable":true},{"id":"F6","severity":"medium","confidence":0.83,"title":"GDELT optionality conflicts with current ML gate required artifacts","location":"/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md Legacy CLI Deprecation and Test Plan; src/quantresearch/preprocessing/production.py:60; tests/test_preprocessing_foundation.py:1951","evidence":"The plan makes GDELT DOC diagnostics optional by default. The current ML gate requires `gdelt_doc_articles.parquet` and `gdelt_extract_audit.csv`; existing tests create empty `not_enabled` artifacts to satisfy the contract.","recommendation":"Define whether skipped GDELT still materializes empty audit files or update the ML gate to accept explicit skipped manifest status.","actionable":true},{"id":"F7","severity":"medium","confidence":0.78,"title":"Endpoint freshness needs completeness semantics, not only date freshness","location":"/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md Date Resolution and Freshness; src/quantresearch/data/backfill.py:538; src/quantresearch/data/backfill.py:547; src/quantresearch/data/integrity.py:242","evidence":"The plan blocks daily execution on stale daily, daily_basic, moneyflow, limit, ETF daily, and benchmarks. Current ingestion has endpoint-specific behavior such as synthesizing moneyflow rows when the endpoint is empty but daily codes exist.","recommendation":"For each freshness family, define expected keys, row-count thresholds, acceptable empty responses, local-first checks, and live-probe fallback behavior.","actionable":true},{"id":"F8","severity":"medium","confidence":0.8,"title":"Checkpoint/resume lacks atomic output and concurrency safeguards","location":"/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md Key Changes checkpoint/resume; src/quantresearch/preprocessing/ml_dataset.py:546; src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_execution_ready_dataset.py:117","evidence":"The plan tracks statuses and hashes, but current writers write directly to final artifact paths. A failed or concurrent run can leave partial files that a hash-based resume system might later consider inputs.","recommendation":"Add run locks, temp output directories, atomic promotion, failed-run quarantine, and reuse only from terminal completed/gated/reused statuses.","actionable":true},{"id":"F9","severity":"medium","confidence":0.74,"title":"Calendar source acquisition and fallback are missing","location":"/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md Date Resolution and Freshness","evidence":"The plan depends on the official SSE calendar as base calendar. The May 1-5, 2026 closure is verified from the official SSE page, but the plan does not define fetch/parser/cache/version/fallback behavior for the official source.","recommendation":"Add a pinned calendar artifact with source URL, retrieved timestamp, parser version, hash, and manual override policy; use Tushare `trade_cal` as cross-check.","actionable":true},{"id":"F10","severity":"low","confidence":0.86,"title":"Mode matrix mixes actions with blocking modifiers","location":"/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md Mode Matrix","evidence":"The valid action list is singular, but table cells use compound expressions such as `execute and block_if_stale`, `probe plus reuse_existing`, and `reuse_existing or block_if_stale`.","recommendation":"Split schema into `action`, `freshness_check`, `blocking_policy`, and `consumers`, or explicitly allow an ordered action list.","actionable":true},{"id":"F11","severity":"low","confidence":0.72,"title":"Active artifact semantics remain unclear","location":"/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md Assumptions; src/quantresearch/preprocessing/research_guardrails.py:22; src/quantresearch/cli/cli_run_factor_search.py:13","evidence":"The plan generates new labels but says canonical research pointers are not updated automatically. Several downstream defaults and guardrail messages still point to the old active ML-ready artifact.","recommendation":"Add an explicit non-goal and operator handoff, or define a separate manual promotion step for active artifacts.","actionable":true}],"unresolved_questions":["Guard rollout soft or hard?","Prediction artifact path name?","Active artifact promotion?","SSE cache source owner?"]}

{"summary":"Subagent result received after final; no code edits made.","markdown_response":"I had already returned the formal JSON review before the second subagent finished. Its findings are consistent with the review I sent: production still shells out and hardcodes `20260424`, ML label cutoff/gate work needs explicit implementation, direct data-writing CLIs remain public/default-driven, resolver/freshness/checkpoint support is mostly absent, and tests/docs need migration coverage.\n\nNo changes were made. The earlier JSON remains the review artifact; the late subagent output does not materially change the conclusions.","findings":[],"unresolved_questions":[]}

items.text: Verify plan file and repo shape
Inspect current production CLI, data CLIs, tests, docs
Verify external calendar/date claim if needed
